description:
The image displays two geographic views, labelled 'a' and 'b', comparing detailed tree detection with a generalized land cover classification. Both views share a common scale bar showing increments of 100 metres up to 500 metres.

View 'a' presents an aerial or satellite orthophoto of a landscape characterised by scattered trees, grasslands, linear features such as roads or paths, and a small water body. Overlaid on this natural-colour imagery are numerous bright green rectangular polygons, which likely represent detected tree crowns or dense tree patches. A few red pixels are also visible, possibly indicating classification anomalies or boundaries.

View 'b' shows a thematic land cover classification of the same geographic area. Large areas are classified as green, representing dense tree cover or general vegetation. Dark grey areas denote other land cover types, which include grasslands, bare soil, and the water body visible in view 'a', as well as roads/paths. Scattered small purple pixels are present, potentially highlighting minor features or local classification discrepancies. This view provides a more generalized, pixelated representation compared to the detailed individual tree detection in view 'a', indicating broader land cover categories or a coarser resolution product.
